Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: JANICE MENDENHALL VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO (D.H.S)
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, December 2, 2014, Line 2, DEFENDANT STEVE ARCELONA'S DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT. Sustained without leave to amend. The Court previously sustained Defendant City and County of San Francisco's demurrer to the Second Amended Complaint ("SAC"), holding that the SAC "does not plead facts establishing Plaintiff complied with the mandatory claims requirement of the Government Claims Act. (Gov. Code sec. 945.4.)" "[A] cause of action against a public employee¿for injury resulting from an act or omission in the scope of his employment as a public employee is barred if an action against the employing public entity for such injury is barred." (Gov. Code sec. 950.2.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: ALLIANCE CREDIT SERVICES INC. VS. MARTIN ENG et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn Anf Mtn To Quash Service And Sanction Pltff'S Mtn For Order To Deem Admitted And To Compel Discovery Responses And Imposing Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, December 2, 2014, Line 1, DEFENDANT MARTIN ENG Motion To Quash Service And Sanction Plaintiff's Motion For Order To Deem Admitted And To Compel Discovery Responses And Imposing Monetary Sanctions. Pro Tem Judge Elizabeth Riles, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Off calendar. This motion is subject to an automatic stay caused by a filing in another court, as per notice filed October 2, 2014. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to eriles@strikebacklaw.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: BITDEFENDER SRL, A ROMANIAN LIMITED LIABILITY VS. 5381 PARTNERS LLC, A DELAWARE LIMITED et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: (Amended) Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Compel Further Responses And For Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, December 2, 2014, Line 2, PLAINTIFF BITDEFENDER SRL, A ROMANIAN LIMITED LIABILITY, BITDEFENDER, LLC, (Amended) Motion To Compel Further Responses And For Monetary Sanctions. Pro Tem Judge Elizabeth Riles, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Motion granted in part and denied in part. With regard to Interrogatory No. 15.1 as to Paragraphs 96, 97, 102, 105, 107 and 108, the motion is granted, Defendant shall provide information whether there were any transfers of assets, consideration paid, etc. With regard to Interrogatory No. 17.1, as to RFA 8, 9, 11, 12, 14 and 15, the motion is granted, Defendant shall state whether it did transfer or commingle for example. With regard to all of the other items, the motion is denied. No sanctions are awarded to either part. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to eriles@strikebacklaw.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: ESTATE OF GENEVA MACK et al VS. PACIFIC PULMONARY SERVICES et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Strike Punitive Damages And The Seventh Cause Of Action For Survival Form The Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, December 2, 2014, Line 7, DEFENDANT INVACARE CORPORATION'S Motion To Strike Punitive Damages And The Seventh Cause Of Action For Survival Form The Complaint. Granted with 10 days leave to amend. The allegations in the complaint do not support a claim for punitive damages. The following paragraphs are stricken from the complaint: 52, 66, 75, and 82. The following words are stricken from paragraph 100: "and exemplary damages" and "penalties, punitive or exemplary damages, pursuant to California Civil Code sec. 3294." The complaint does not plead facts establishing that Defendant acted with "oppression, fraud, or malice" as required by Civ. Code sec. 3294(a). (See Grieves v. Sup. Ct. (1984) 157 Cal.App.3d 159, 166, "Not only must there be circumstances of oppression, fraud, or malice, but facts must be alleged in the pleading to support such a claim.") The complaint also does not plead facts establishing an officer, director or managing agent of the corporation "had advance knowledge of the unfitness of the employee and employed him with a conscious disregard of the rights or safety of others or authorized or ratified the wrongful conduct," as required by Civ. Code sec. 3294(b). (See Cruz v. HomeBase (2000) 83 Cal.App.4th 160, 167, "Corporations are legal entities which do not have minds capable of recklessness, wickedness, or intent to injure or deceive. An award of punitive damages against a corporation therefore must rest on the malice of the corporation's employees.") Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)
